Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

I'm not clear at what the idea is for these scripts.

Are they just random scripts that you think other people might find handy, but shouldn't be packaged or installed on system?  Then the proper place to put these would be the contrib subdirectory.

Are these supposed to be installed and make Lustre's tool set more complete and consistent?  In that case they really need work.  These script names are really too vague for packaging and installation.  And perhaps they shouldn't be separate scripts, but improvements to lfs or lctl, or whatever.

In any event, I see no changes to an .am files, so I doubt these are properly packaged, even just with appropriate EXTRA_DIST (Note that the contrib directory is one big blanket EXTRA_DIST, more-or-less, so you wouldn't need to edit any makefiles if you put them there.)

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

I think they're a better logical fit with scripts like lustre_rmmod than with checkpatch.pl

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

"I think they're a better logical fit with scripts like lustre_rmmod than with checkpatch.pl"

No.  lustre_rmmod is an _installed_ script that is used by things like init and systemd scripts.

These scripts, you are just including as handy scripts in the source tree that other people might like.  That means they got in contrib/scripts/.

Patch Set 7:

These scripts are also meant to be installed and used by both site admins and tools that are built for managing Lustre storage.  Merely parking them in the source tree will guarantee that they will never be used by anyone.  As LU-4939 clearly lays out, there is a long-standing demand for this from actual users.

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

"Merely parking them in the source tree will guarantee that they will never be used by anyone."

OK, then why are you merely parking them in the source tree?  You didn't install or package your scripts.  Is that a bug?

The bigger question, before you try to install and package them, is whether this approach is the right one for lustre?  I would argue that an explosion of random helper scripts is not the way to define a clean interface to a piece of software.  We have commands like lctl and lfs.  Can they not be improved and expanded to do what we need?

Next, if it is decided that a proliferation of scripts in /usr/{s}bin really is the right approach, then these scripts are still really ready to be placed there.  Reasons:

Only two of the three scripts have a "usage" function, and none of them actually explain what they do.
None of them accept a "-h" option.
No comment at the top of the script explaining its function.
No man pages.

The standard for installed programs is (or should be) higher than just sharing a script in contrib.  And we need to consider if the script style fits into an overall model of user interface, or is just something random added on the side.

parents.subject: LU-10443 test: Handle file lifecycle correctly

commit.subject: LU-4939 utils: Add scripts to output MGS config parameters
commit.message:
LU-4939 utils: Add scripts to output MGS config parameters

Add config output utilities to lctl.  Print a list of steps to
re-create any config parameters, OST pools and quota info.

These utilities are helpful in migrating configurations, or backing
up and restoring configurations during a writeconf.

usage: lctl --device [device] config_print <fsname>
lctl pool_list -c <fsname>

Signed-off-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Change-Id: I7ceffea2eddecc0585d075b61cab8049616817ae
Cray-bug-id: LUS-986
